BLite.Server

Preview
🖥️Overview📦Installation🔌Protocol & API🎨Web Studio📡Client SDKs🔗Version Matrix

🎨 Web Studio

BLite.Server ships a built-in browser-based management UI — built with Blazor Server — for browsing databases, editing documents, running queries, and managing users.

Enabling the Studio

The Studio is disabled by default in production Docker images. Enable it in appsettings.json:

{
  "Studio": {
    "Enabled": true
  }
}

Or via environment variable:

Studio__Enabled=true

Once enabled, the Studio is served on port 2628 (configurable via Kestrel:Endpoints:Studio).

ℹ Security: The Studio requires its own Admin API key to log in — separate from the API key used by your application. Keep the Studio port off the public internet unless you need remote admin access.

First-Run Setup

On the very first launch with no users configured, navigate to:

https://localhost:2628/setup

The setup wizard will:

  1. Prompt you to create the root admin user with a username and a generated API key.
  2. Display the plaintext API key once — copy and store it immediately. It cannot be recovered.
  3. Mark the server as configured, disabling Dev Mode permanently.
⚠ Save your key immediately. The plaintext API key is shown exactly once. If you lose it, you must rotate the key via the Studio or the POST /api/v1/users/{username}/rotate REST endpoint.

Logging In

After setup is complete, navigate to:

https://localhost:2628/login

Enter your username and API key. The Studio authenticates against the BLite permission system — only users with Admin permission on "*" (all collections) can log into the Studio.

Studio authentication is session-scoped (Blazor Server circuit). Closing the tab or refreshing the page logs you out — no persistent browser cookies are involved.

Features

Dashboard

The home screen shows a summary of all registered databases, total collection count, and server uptime.

Databases

Browse all tenant databases. Create new databases and drop existing ones (requires Admin permission). Each database displays its file size and collection count.

Collections & Documents

Select any collection to open a two-panel view:

  • Left panel: paginated document list with ID, a preview of key fields, and quick-delete buttons.
  • Right panel: a full JSON/BSON document editor. Insert new documents or edit existing ones — changes are saved on confirmation.

A built-in BLQL query bar lets you filter the list without leaving the page.

Users

The Users page (Admin only) allows you to:

  • List all users with their namespace, database isolation, and permission summary.
  • Create new users with fine-grained per-collection permissions.
  • Rotate an existing user's API key (old key is invalidated immediately).
  • Delete users.

System Info

Displays the connected BLite engine version, server uptime, and the active configuration paths. Useful for verifying the correct data directory is in use.

Navigation Structure

URLPageDescription
/setupSetup WizardFirst-run only; creates root user
/loginLoginStudio authentication
/DashboardServer status overview
/databasesDatabasesManage tenant databases
/collectionsCollectionsBrowse collections within a database
/documentsDocumentsView, edit, and query documents
/usersUsersUser and permission management

Security Notes

  • Studio authentication is independent from API-level authentication. Even if a Studio session is active, gRPC/REST requests still require a valid x-api-key header.
  • The Studio is served over the same TLS certificate as the REST API. In development, this is a self-signed cert — you will need to accept the browser warning or trust the cert in your OS store.
  • Collections whose names start with _ are reserved and hidden from non-Admin users. The Studio only shows them when logged in as Admin.

"Sky is Blite, no Clouds today."
